intro: Rhaphidospora glabra is a plant species that belongs to the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the mangosteen family. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Rhaphidospora glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Rhaphidospora glabra is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is commonly consumed as a tea or used topically as a paste or ointment to treat various health ailments.
